title: Blocker Decomposition
description: Use the node exporter's split-frames feature to decompose blocker.py (~950 lines) into a folder structure like neck/. The blocker has 6+ top-level frames with significant cross-boundary dependencies. May require manual cleanup of the exported output to match coding standards.
definition-of-done: 1. blocker.py replaced with blocker/ folder. 2. Each top-level frame in its own @geo_node_group file. 3. main.py orchestrates sub-groups. 4. All files use node_helpers where applicable. 5. Geometry unchanged vs original.
testing-plan: blender-cli validate --group Blocker before and after. Compare mesh metrics (vertex/face count).
todos: TODO : Export Blocker with split_frames + use_helpers via node exporter
TODO : Review generated output for correctness and coding standards
TODO : Clean up variable names and add missing helpers
TODO : Replace blocker.py with blocker/ folder
unknowns: How tangled are the cross-frame dependencies in practice?
Will the auto-generated interface sockets match readable naming?
